input.prompt:
I need to understand the unsloth codebase structure, specifically:

1. Find all files related to saving/exporting models (GGUF export, merged model saving, adapter saving)
2. Understand how save methods are organized and exposed
3. Find where LoRA adapter weights (A and B matrices) are accessed
4. Find the main model classes and how save methods are registered/exposed

Be very thorough. Search for:
- Files with "save" in name
- Files with "export" in name  
- Files with "gguf" in name
- Grep for "save_pretrained", "push_to_hub", "save_model", "export"
- Grep for "lora_A", "lora_B" to find where adapter matrices are accessed
- Look at the top-level package structure

Report file paths, key class names, method signatures, and how the save/export pipeline works.

159	def _merge_lora(layer, name):
160	
161	    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
162	    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
163	        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
164	        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
165	        if quant_state is not None:
166	            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
167	            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
168	        else:
169	            dtype = W.dtype
170	        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
171	        # W = W.t()
172	
173	        if A is not None:
174	            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
175	            # W += sAB
176	            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
177	            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
178	            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
179	            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
180	            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
181	                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
182	        pass
183	        W = W.t().to(dtype)
184	    else:
185	        W = layer.weight
186	    return W, bias
187	pass

770	def install_llama_cpp_make_non_blocking():
771	    # https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp/issues/7062
772	    # Weirdly GPU conversion for GGUF breaks??
773	    # env = { **os.environ, "LLAMA_CUDA": "1", }
774	    # Force make clean
775	    check = os.system("make clean -C llama.cpp")
776	    IS_CMAKE = False
777	    if check == 0:
778	        # Uses old MAKE
779	        n_jobs = max(int(psutil.cpu_count()*1.5), 1)
780	        full_command = ["make", "all", "-j"+str(n_jobs), "-C", "llama.cpp"]
781	        IS_CMAKE = False
782	    else:
783	        # Uses new CMAKE
784	        n_jobs = max(int(psutil.cpu_count()), 1) # Use less CPUs since 1.5x faster
785	        check = os.system("cmake llama.cpp -B llama.cpp/build -DBUILD_SHARED_LIBS=OFF -DGGML_CUDA=OFF -DLLAMA_CURL=ON")
786	        if check != 0:
787	            raise RuntimeError(f"*** Unsloth: Failed compiling llama.cpp using os.system(...) with error {check}. Please report this ASAP!")
788	        pass
789	        # f"cmake --build llama.cpp/build --config Release -j{psutil.cpu_count()*2} --clean-first --target {' '.join(LLAMA_CPP_TARGETS)}",
790	        full_command = [
791	            "cmake", "--build", "llama.cpp/build",
792	            "--config", "Release",
793	            "-j"+str(n_jobs),
794	            "--clean-first",
795	            "--target",
796	        ] + LLAMA_CPP_TARGETS
797	        IS_CMAKE = True
798	    pass
799	    # https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp/issues/7062
800	    # Weirdly GPU conversion for GGUF breaks??

814	def try_execute(commands, force_complete = False):
815	    for command in commands:
816	        with subprocess.Popen(command, shell = True, stdout = subprocess.PIPE, stderr = subprocess.STDOUT, bufsize = 1) as sp:
817	            for line in sp.stdout:
818	                line = line.decode("utf-8", errors = "replace")
819	                if "undefined reference" in line:
820	                    raise RuntimeError(f"*** Unsloth: Failed compiling llama.cpp with {line}. Please report this ASAP!")
821	                elif "deprecated" in line:
822	                    return "CMAKE"
823	                elif "Unknown argument" in line:
824	                    raise RuntimeError(f"*** Unsloth: Failed compiling llama.cpp with {line}. Please report this ASAP!")
825	                elif "***" in line:
826	                    raise RuntimeError(f"*** Unsloth: Failed compiling llama.cpp with {line}. Please report this ASAP!")
827	                print(line, flush = True, end = "")
828	            pass
829	            if force_complete and sp.returncode is not None and sp.returncode != 0:
830	                raise subprocess.CalledProcessError(sp.returncode, sp.args)
831	        pass
832	    pass
833	    return None

190	def get_lora_parameters(proj):
191	    """
192	    Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale).
193	    If QAT is enabled, additionally fake quantize the base layer and lora weights.
194	    """
195	    # For DPO or disabled adapters
196	    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
197	    W = base_layer.weight
198	
199	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to base layer weights for QAT
200	    weight_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
201	    if weight_fake_quantizer is not None:
202	        W = weight_fake_quantizer(W)
203	
204	    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
205	    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
206	        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None
207	    pass
208	
209	    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
210	    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
211	    adapter = adapter[0]
212	
213	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to lora weights for QAT
214	    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
215	    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
216	    lora_A_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_A_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
217	    lora_B_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_B_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
218	    A = lora_A_linear.weight
219	    B = lora_B_linear.weight
220	    if lora_A_fake_quantizer is not None:
221	        A = lora_A_fake_quantizer(A)
222	    if lora_B_fake_quantizer is not None:
223	        B = lora_B_fake_quantizer(B)
224	
225	    return (
226	        W,
227	        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
228	        A,
229	        B,
230	        proj.scaling[adapter],
231	    )
232	pass

276	    @torch.inference_mode
277	    def fast_dequantize(W, quant_state = None, out = None, use_global_buffer = False):
278	        # TODO: After adding XPU BNB support, check this function
279	        if quant_state is None: return W
280	        if type(quant_state) is not list:
281	            # New quant_state as a class
282	            # https://github.com/TimDettmers/bitsandbytes/pull/763/files
283	            absmax     = quant_state.absmax
284	            shape      = quant_state.shape
285	            dtype      = quant_state.dtype
286	            blocksize  = quant_state.blocksize
287	            offset     = quant_state.offset
288	            state2     = quant_state.state2
289	            absmax2    = state2.absmax
290	            code2      = state2.code
291	            blocksize2 = state2.blocksize
292	        else:
293	            # Old quant_state as a list of lists
294	            absmax, shape, dtype, blocksize, compressed_stats, _, _ = quant_state
295	            offset, state2 = compressed_stats
296	            absmax2, code2, blocksize2, _, _, _, _ = state2
297	        pass
298	        global XPU_STREAMS
299	        device = W.device
300	        device_index = device.index
301	        XPU_STREAM = XPU_STREAMS[device_index]

303	        n_elements_absmax = absmax.numel()
304	        # Create weight matrix
305	        if use_global_buffer:
306	
307	            # Use same buffers for faster inference
308	            size = shape[0]*shape[1]
309	            global WEIGHT_BUFFERS
310	            global ABSMAX_BUFFERS
311	            WEIGHT_BUFFER = WEIGHT_BUFFERS[device_index]
312	            ABSMAX_BUFFER = ABSMAX_BUFFERS[device_index]
313	            if WEIGHT_BUFFER is None:
314	                WEIGHT_BUFFERS[device_index] = WEIGHT_BUFFER = torch_empty(size, dtype = dtype, device = device, requires_grad = False)
315	                ABSMAX_BUFFERS[device_index] = ABSMAX_BUFFER = torch_empty(n_elements_absmax, dtype = torch.float32, device = device, requires_grad = False)
316	
317	            if size > WEIGHT_BUFFER.numel(): WEIGHT_BUFFER.resize_(size)
318	            if n_elements_absmax > ABSMAX_BUFFER.numel(): ABSMAX_BUFFER.resize_(n_elements_absmax)
319	
320	            out = WEIGHT_BUFFER[:size].view(shape)
321	            out_absmax = ABSMAX_BUFFER[:n_elements_absmax]
322	        else:
323	            if out is None:
324	                out = torch_empty(shape, dtype = dtype, device = device, requires_grad = False)
325	            else:
326	                assert(out.shape == shape)
327	                assert(out.dtype == dtype)
328	            out_absmax = torch_empty(n_elements_absmax, dtype = torch_float32, device = device, requires_grad = False)
329	        pass
330	
331	        # NF4 dequantization of statistics
332	        ptr_out_absmax = get_ptr(out_absmax)
333	        with torch_gpu_device(device):
334	            cdequantize_blockwise_fp32(
335	                get_ptr(code2), get_ptr(absmax), get_ptr(absmax2), ptr_out_absmax,
336	                ctypes_c_int(blocksize2), ctypes_c_int(n_elements_absmax), XPU_STREAM
337	            )
338	            out_absmax += offset

28	class LoRA_MLP(torch.autograd.Function):
29	    """
30	    ### LoRA weights
31	    G = G + Ag @ Bg
32	    U = U + Au @ Bu
33	    W = W + Aw @ Bw
34	
35	    ### SwiGLU(X)
36	    e = X @ G
37	    f = e * sigmoid(e)
38	    g = X @ U
39	    h = f * g
40	    i = h @ W
41	
42	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
43	    See our blog post for more details
44	
45	    df = sigmoid(e) * (1 - f) + f
46	    dC/dW = h.T @ dY
47	    dC/dU = X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
48	    dC/dG = X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
49	
50	    ### Down projection LoRA weights
51	    dC/dAw = dC/dW @ B.T
52	    dC/dBw = A.T @ dC/dW
53	    dC/dAw =       h.T @ dY @ B.T
54	    dC/dBw = A.T @ h.T @ dY
55	
56	    ### Up projection LoRA weights
57	    dC/dAu =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * f) @ B.T
58	    dC/dBu = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
59	
60	    ### Gate projection LoRA weights
61	    dC/dAg =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g) @ B.T
62	    dC/dBg = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
63	
64	    Don't forget to see our blog post for more details!
65	    """
66	    @staticmethod
67	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
68	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
69	                gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
70	                  upW,   upW_quant, upA,   upB,   upS,
71	                downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
72	                _forward_function, _backward_function,
73	                inplace = True,):
74	        dtype = X.dtype
75	
76	        e = matmul_lora(X, gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS)
77	        g = matmul_lora(X,   upW,   upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS)
78	        h = _forward_function(e, g)
79	        i = matmul_lora(h, downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS)
80	
81	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
82	            gateW, gateW_quant, gateS,
83	            upW, upW_quant, upS,
84	            downW, downW_quant, downS,
85	            _backward_function,
86	        )
87	        ctx.save_for_backward(gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB,
88	                              X, e, g)
89	        ctx.inplace = inplace
90	        return i
91	    pass
92	
93	
94	    @staticmethod
95	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
96	    def backward(ctx, dY : torch.Tensor):
97	        gateW, gateW_quant, gateS, upW, upW_quant, upS, downW, downW_quant, downS, \
98	            _backward_function = ctx.custom_saved_tensors
99	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB, \
100	            X, e, g = ctx.saved_tensors
101	
102	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
103	        dY = dY.view(-1, dY.shape[-1])
104	        X  = X .view(-1, X .shape[-1])
105	        e  = e .view(-1, e .shape[-1])
106	        g  = g .view(-1, g .shape[-1])
107	        dtype = X.dtype
108	
109	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB = \
110	            gateA.to(dtype), gateB.to(dtype), upA.to(dtype), upB.to(dtype), downA.to(dtype), downB.to(dtype)
111	
112	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB = \
113	            gateA.t(), gateB.t(), upA.t(), upB.t(), downA.t(), downB.t()
114	
115	        DW = matmul_lora(dY, downW.t(), downW_quant, downB, downA, downS)
116	        DW, e, g = _backward_function(DW, e, g)
117	        h, df, de = DW, e, g
118	
119	        d_downA = torch.empty_like(downA)
120	        d_downB = torch.empty_like(downB)
121	        d_gateA = torch.empty_like(gateA)
122	        d_gateB = torch.empty_like(gateB)
123	        d_upA   = torch.empty_like(upA)
124	        d_upB   = torch.empty_like(upB)
125	
126	        # Down projection LoRA weights
127	        # d_downA = h.t() @ (dY @ downB.t())
128	        # d_downB = (downA.t() @ h.t()) @ dY
129	        # d_downA *= downS
130	        # d_downB *= downS
131	        d_downA.addmm_(h.t(), dY @ downB.t(), alpha = downS, beta = 0)
132	        d_downB.addmm_(downA.t() @ h.t(), dY, alpha = downS, beta = 0)
133	
134	        # Up projection LoRA weights
135	        # d_upA   = X.t() @ (df @ upB.t())
136	        # d_upB   = (upA.t() @ X.t()) @ df
137	        # d_upA  *= upS
138	        # d_upB  *= upS
139	        d_upA.addmm_(X.t(), df @ upB.t(), alpha = upS, beta = 0)
140	        d_upB.addmm_(upA.t() @ X.t(), df, alpha = upS, beta = 0)
141	
142	        # Gate projection LoRA weights
143	        # d_gateA = X.t() @ (de @ gateB.t())
144	        # d_gateB = (gateA.t() @ X.t()) @ de
145	        # d_gateA *= gateS
146	        # d_gateB *= gateS
147	        d_gateA.addmm_(X.t(), de @ gateB.t(), alpha = gateS, beta = 0)
148	        d_gateB.addmm_(gateA.t() @ X.t(), de, alpha = gateS, beta = 0)
149	
150	        # dX  = matmul_lora(df, upW.t(), upW_quant, upB, upA, upS)
151	        # dX += matmul_lora(de, gateW.t(), gateW_quant, gateB, gateA, gateS)
152	        upW = fast_dequantize(upW.t(), upW_quant)
153	        dX = torch.matmul(df, upW.t(), out = X if ctx.inplace else None)
154	        del upW
155	        # dX += df @ upB.to(dtype).t() @ (upS * upA.to(dtype).t())
156	        dX.addmm_(df @ upB.t(), upA.t(), alpha = upS)
157	
158	        gateW = fast_dequantize(gateW.t(), gateW_quant)
159	        # dX += de @ gateW.t()
160	        dX.addmm_(de, gateW.t())
161	        del gateW
162	        # dX += de @ gateB.to(dtype).t() @ (gateS * gateA.to(dtype).t())
163	        dX.addmm_(de @ gateB.t(), gateA.t(), alpha = gateS)
164	
165	        # gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
166	        #  upW,    upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS,
167	        # downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
168	        return dX.view(batch, seq_len, hd), \

Now I have enough information. Let me create a comprehensive report on the unsloth codebase structure.

## Comprehensive Unsloth Codebase Structure Report

### 1. TOP-LEVEL PACKAGE STRUCTURE

**Root directory:**
- `/unsloth/` - Main package directory
  - `__init__.py` - Package initialization; imports all public APIs
  - `save.py` - Core save/export functionality (105KB file)
  - `chat_templates.py` - Chat template handling
  - `tokenizer_utils.py` - Tokenizer utilities
  - `trainer.py` - Trainer patches
  - `models/` - Model-specific implementations
  - `kernels/` - CUDA/GPU kernels
  - `registry/` - Model registry
  - `utils/` - Utility functions
  - `dataprep/` - Data preparation utilities

---

### 2. SAVE/EXPORT FUNCTIONALITY FILES

**Main save module:** `/unsloth/save.py`

This file is the central hub for all model saving and exporting. Key exports defined in `__all__`:
- `print_quantization_methods`
- `unsloth_save_model`
- `save_to_gguf`
- `patch_saving_functions`
- `create_huggingface_repo`

**Test files for save functionality:**
- `/tests/saving/test_unsloth_save.py` - Main save tests
- `/tests/saving/language_models/` - Model-specific saving tests
  - `test_merge_4bit_validation.py`
  - `test_merge_model_perplexity_*.py`
  - `test_push_to_hub_merged.py`
  - `test_push_to_hub_merged_sharded_index_file.py`
  - `test_save_merged_grpo_model.py`

---

### 3. CORE SAVE/EXPORT FUNCTION SIGNATURES

All save functions are defined in `/unsloth/save.py`:

#### A. **Main Save Function**

```python
def unsloth_save_model(
    model,
    tokenizer,
    save_directory: Union[str, os.PathLike],
    save_method: str = "lora",  # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    push_to_hub: bool = False,
    token: Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    is_main_process: bool = True,
    state_dict: Optional[dict] = None,
    save_function: Callable = torch.save,
    max_shard_size: Union[int, str] = "5GB",
    safe_serialization: bool = True,
    variant: Optional[str] = None,
    save_peft_format: bool = True,
    # Push to hub parameters
    use_temp_dir: Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message: Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",
    private: Optional[bool] = None,
    create_pr: bool = False,
    revision: str = None,
    commit_description: str = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",
    tags: List[str] = None,
    # Memory parameters
    temporary_location: str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage: float = 0.9,
) -> Tuple[str, Optional[str]]
```

**Location:** Line 205-760

**Save methods:**
- `"lora"` - Saves only LoRA adapters (fastest, smallest)
- `"merged_16bit"` - Merges LoRA into float16 weights (for GGUF/llama.cpp)
- `"merged_4bit"` - Merges LoRA into int4 weights (for DPO/inference)

#### B. **Merged Model Saving Methods (Exposed to Models)**

---

### 4. HOW SAVE METHODS ARE REGISTERED/EXPOSED

**Function:** `patch_saving_functions(model, vision=False)`  
**Location:** Line 2569

This function dynamically patches model instances with save methods:

```python
def patch_saving_functions(model, vision=False):
    # Patches the model.push_to_hub method
    # For language models, adds these methods:
    if not vision:
        if hasattr(model, "config"):
            model.push_to_hub_merged = types.MethodType(
                unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged, model)
            model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(
                unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
            model.push_to_hub_gguf = types.MethodType(
                unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf, model)
            model.save_pretrained_gguf = types.MethodType(
                unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf, model)
            model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(
                unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao, model)
            model.push_to_hub_ggml = types.MethodType(
                unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub, model)
            model.save_pretrained_ggml = types.MethodType(
                unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)
    # For vision models, only merged saving is supported
    else:
        model.push_to_hub_merged = types.MethodType(
            unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged, model)
        model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(
            unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
        # Other methods disabled for vision
```

**Where patch_saving_functions is called:**
- `/unsloth/models/llama.py` - Lines 2188, 2204, 2902 (after loading models)
- `/unsloth/models/vision.py` - Lines 702, 703, 918, 964 (after loading vision models)

### 5. CORE MODEL CLASSES

**Main model classes:** `/unsloth/models/loader.py`

---

### 6. LORA ADAPTER WEIGHTS ACCESS

**File:** `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py`

#### A. **Get LoRA Parameters**

```python
def get_lora_parameters(proj):
    """
    Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale).
    """
    # Line 190
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
    W = base_layer.weight
    
    # Get active adapter
    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: 
        adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]
    
    # Access LoRA A and B matrices
    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]  # Dict-like access by adapter name
    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
    A = lora_A_linear.weight
    B = lora_B_linear.weight
    
    return (
        W,                          # Base weight
        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),  # Quantization state
        A,                          # LoRA A matrix
        B,                          # LoRA B matrix
        proj.scaling[adapter],      # LoRA scaling factor
    )
```

#### B. **Get LoRA Parameters with Bias**

```python
def get_lora_parameters_bias(proj):
    """Same as above but also returns bias."""
    # Line 235
    return (
        W,                          # Base weight
        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
        proj.lora_A[adapter].weight,  # LoRA A matrix (via .weight attr)
        proj.lora_B[adapter].weight,  # LoRA B matrix (via .weight attr)
        proj.scaling[adapter],        # Scaling factor
        base_layer.bias,              # Bias term
    )
```

**LoRA matrix properties:**
- `proj.lora_A[adapter_name]` - Dict-like access to LoRA A linear layer
- `proj.lora_B[adapter_name]` - Dict-like access to LoRA B linear layer
- `proj.lora_A[adapter_name].weight` - Actual A matrix tensor
- `proj.lora_B[adapter_name].weight` - Actual B matrix tensor
- `proj.scaling[adapter_name]` - Scaling factor (usually alpha/r)

---

### 7. MERGING LORA WEIGHTS INTO BASE WEIGHTS

**File:** `/unsloth/save.py`, Lines 159-187

```python
def _merge_lora(layer, name):
    """Merge LoRA weights into base weights."""
    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
        # Get LoRA parameters
        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
        
        # Dequantize if quantized
        if quant_state is not None:
            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
        else:
            dtype = W.dtype
        
        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
        
        # Merge: W += A.T @ (s * B.T)
        if A is not None:
            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha=s)
        
        W = W.t().to(dtype)
    else:
        W = layer.weight
    return W, bias
```

**Key merge operation:** `W += scaling * (A.T @ B.T)`

---

### 8. STATE DICT CONSTRUCTION FOR MERGED MODELS

**Location:** `/unsloth/save.py`, Lines 549-619

Process for merged_16bit saves:
1. Create OrderedDict for state_dict
2. Determine model dtype from config
3. Extract embedding weights
4. Iterate through model layers:
   - For each weight matrix (q_proj, k_proj, v_proj, o_proj, gate_proj, up_proj, down_proj):
     - Merge LoRA weights using `_merge_lora()`
     - Handle biases
     - Save to GPU memory (if space available) or disk
   - For layernorms: copy weights directly (no LoRA)
5. Include norm weights and LM head weights
6. Handle tied weights (embedding/LM head)

---

### 9. SAVING PIPELINE FLOW

```
unsloth_save_model()
├─ If save_method == "merged_4bit":
│  └─ Call model.merge_and_unload()
│
├─ If push_to_hub for LoRA/4bit:
│  └─ Call model.push_to_hub() directly
│
├─ If save_method == "lora":
│  └─ Call model.save_pretrained()
│
└─ If save_method == "merged_16bit" (complex path):
   ├─ Merge LoRA weights layer by layer
   ├─ Construct state_dict with merged weights
   ├─ Remove quantization_config from config
   ├─ Call internal_model.save_pretrained() with custom state_dict
   └─ Restore original config

save_to_gguf()
├─ Install llama.cpp if needed
├─ Call unsloth_save_model() with save_method="merged_16bit"
├─ Use convert-hf-to-gguf.py to create GGUF
└─ Use quantize tool for each quantization method
```

---

### 10. KEY IMPORTS AND DEPENDENCIES

**Core imports in save.py:**
```python
from unsloth_zoo.utils import Version
from unsloth_zoo.hf_utils import dtype_from_config, HAS_TORCH_DTYPE
from bitsandbytes.nn import Linear4bit as Bnb_Linear4bit
from peft.tuners.lora import Linear4bit as Peft_Linear4bit
from peft.tuners.lora import Linear as Peft_Linear
from .kernels import fast_dequantize, QUANT_STATE, get_lora_parameters_bias
from huggingface_hub import HfApi, get_token
```

**Key exports in package __init__.py:**
```python

from .save import *
from .chat_templates import *
from .tokenizer_utils import *
from .trainer import *
```

---

### 11. SUPPORTED MODELS FOR SAVE/EXPORT

Language models with full save support:
- Llama (all versions: 2, 3, 3.1, 3.2)
- Mistral
- Qwen2, Qwen2.5, Qwen3, Qwen3-MoE
- Gemma, Gemma2
- Granite
- Cohere
- Phi

Vision models (merged saving only):
- Llama-Vision
- Qwen2.5-VL
- Gemma-3 (with vision)

---

### 12. MEMORY AND PERFORMANCE CONSIDERATIONS

**Configuration parameters in save.py:**
- `maximum_memory_usage` (default 0.9) - Fraction of available memory to use
- `max_shard_size` (default "5GB") - Size for model sharding
- `safe_serialization` (default True) - Use safetensors or PyTorch format
- Automatic fallback to disk when VRAM/RAM insufficient
- Optional safe_serialization disabling on low-CPU systems

---

### 13. EXAMPLE USAGE PATTERNS

From test file (`/tests/saving/test_unsloth_save.py`):

```python
# Load and prepare model
model, tokenizer = FastModel.from_pretrained(
    "unsloth/tinyllama",
    max_seq_length=128,
    load_in_4bit=True,
)

model = FastModel.get_peft_model(
    model,
    r=16,
    target_modules=["q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", "o_proj"],
    lora_alpha=16,
)

# Save merged 16-bit (for GGUF)
model.save_pretrained_merged(
    "path/to/save",
    tokenizer=tokenizer,
    save_method="merged_16bit"
)

# Push to Hub
model.push_to_hub_merged(
    "username/model_name",
    tokenizer=tokenizer,
    save_method="merged_16bit"
)

# Save as GGUF
model.save_pretrained_gguf(
    "path/to/save",
    tokenizer=tokenizer,
    quantization_method="q4_k_m"
)

# Save with TorchAO
model.save_pretrained_torchao(
    "path/to/save",
    tokenizer=tokenizer,
)
```

---

## Summary

The unsloth save/export architecture is well-organized:

1. **Single entry point** (`unsloth_save_model`) handles all save logic internally
2. **Exposed methods** are dynamically patched onto models via `patch_saving_functions`
3. **LoRA access** is clean: `proj.lora_A[adapter_name].weight` and `proj.lora_B[adapter_name].weight`
4. **Merging** uses the formula: `W += scaling * (A.T @ B.T)`
5. **Export options**: LoRA-only, merged 16-bit, merged 4-bit, GGUF, GGML, TorchAO
6. **Memory management** is sophisticated with fallback to disk when needed
7. **HuggingFace integration** is seamless with automatic tokenizer handling

Now I have enough information. Let me create a comprehensive report on the unsloth codebase structure.

## Comprehensive Unsloth Codebase Structure Report

### 1. TOP-LEVEL PACKAGE STRUCTURE

**Root directory:**
- `/unsloth/` - Main package directory
  - `__init__.py` - Package initialization; imports all public APIs
  - `save.py` - Core save/export functionality (105KB file)
  - `chat_templates.py` - Chat template handling
  - `tokenizer_utils.py` - Tokenizer utilities
  - `trainer.py` - Trainer patches
  - `models/` - Model-specific implementations
  - `kernels/` - CUDA/GPU kernels
  - `registry/` - Model registry
  - `utils/` - Utility functions
  - `dataprep/` - Data preparation utilities

---

### 2. SAVE/EXPORT FUNCTIONALITY FILES

**Main save module:** `/unsloth/save.py`

This file is the central hub for all model saving and exporting. Key exports defined in `__all__`:
- `print_quantization_methods`
- `unsloth_save_model`
- `save_to_gguf`
- `patch_saving_functions`
- `create_huggingface_repo`

**Test files for save functionality:**
- `/tests/saving/test_unsloth_save.py` - Main save tests
- `/tests/saving/language_models/` - Model-specific saving tests
  - `test_merge_4bit_validation.py`
  - `test_merge_model_perplexity_*.py`
  - `test_push_to_hub_merged.py`
  - `test_push_to_hub_merged_sharded_index_file.py`
  - `test_save_merged_grpo_model.py`

---

### 3. CORE SAVE/EXPORT FUNCTION SIGNATURES

All save functions are defined in `/unsloth/save.py`:

#### A. **Main Save Function**

```python
def unsloth_save_model(
    model,
    tokenizer,
    save_directory: Union[str, os.PathLike],
    save_method: str = "lora",  # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    push_to_hub: bool = False,
    token: Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    is_main_process: bool = True,
    state_dict: Optional[dict] = None,
    save_function: Callable = torch.save,
    max_shard_size: Union[int, str] = "5GB",
    safe_serialization: bool = True,
    variant: Optional[str] = None,
    save_peft_format: bool = True,
    # Push to hub parameters
    use_temp_dir: Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message: Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",
    private: Optional[bool] = None,
    create_pr: bool = False,
    revision: str = None,
    commit_description: str = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",
    tags: List[str] = None,
    # Memory parameters
    temporary_location: str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage: float = 0.9,
) -> Tuple[str, Optional[str]]
```

**Location:** Line 205-760

**Save methods:**
- `"lora"` - Saves only LoRA adapters (fastest, smallest)
- `"merged_16bit"` - Merges LoRA into float16 weights (for GGUF/llama.cpp)
- `"merged_4bit"` - Merges LoRA into int4 weights (for DPO/inference)

#### B. **Merged Model Saving Methods (Exposed to Models)**

---

### 4. HOW SAVE METHODS ARE REGISTERED/EXPOSED

**Function:** `patch_saving_functions(model, vision=False)`  
**Location:** Line 2569

This function dynamically patches model instances with save methods:

```python
def patch_saving_functions(model, vision=False):
    # Patches the model.push_to_hub method
    # For language models, adds these methods:
    if not vision:
        if hasattr(model, "config"):
            model.push_to_hub_merged = types.MethodType(
                unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged, model)
            model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(
                unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
            model.push_to_hub_gguf = types.MethodType(
                unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf, model)
            model.save_pretrained_gguf = types.MethodType(
                unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf, model)
            model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(
                unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao, model)
            model.push_to_hub_ggml = types.MethodType(
                unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub, model)
            model.save_pretrained_ggml = types.MethodType(
                unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)
    # For vision models, only merged saving is supported
    else:
        model.push_to_hub_merged = types.MethodType(
            unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged, model)
        model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(
            unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
        # Other methods disabled for vision
```

**Where patch_saving_functions is called:**
- `/unsloth/models/llama.py` - Lines 2188, 2204, 2902 (after loading models)
- `/unsloth/models/vision.py` - Lines 702, 703, 918, 964 (after loading vision models)
- `/unsloth/chat_templates.py` - Line 2309 (for tokenizers)

- `FastFalconH1Model` - `/unsloth/models/falcon_h1.py` (Transformers >= 4.53)

---

### 6. LORA ADAPTER WEIGHTS ACCESS

**File:** `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py`

#### A. **Get LoRA Parameters**

```python
def get_lora_parameters(proj):
    """
    Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale).
    """
    # Line 190
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
    W = base_layer.weight
    
    # Get active adapter
    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: 
        adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]
    
    # Access LoRA A and B matrices
    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]  # Dict-like access by adapter name
    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
    A = lora_A_linear.weight
    B = lora_B_linear.weight
    
    return (
        W,                          # Base weight
        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),  # Quantization state
        A,                          # LoRA A matrix
        B,                          # LoRA B matrix
        proj.scaling[adapter],      # LoRA scaling factor
    )
```

#### B. **Get LoRA Parameters with Bias**

```python
def get_lora_parameters_bias(proj):
    """Same as above but also returns bias."""
    # Line 235
    return (
        W,                          # Base weight
        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
        proj.lora_A[adapter].weight,  # LoRA A matrix (via .weight attr)
        proj.lora_B[adapter].weight,  # LoRA B matrix (via .weight attr)
        proj.scaling[adapter],        # Scaling factor
        base_layer.bias,              # Bias term
    )
```

**LoRA matrix properties:**
- `proj.lora_A[adapter_name]` - Dict-like access to LoRA A linear layer
- `proj.lora_B[adapter_name]` - Dict-like access to LoRA B linear layer
- `proj.lora_A[adapter_name].weight` - Actual A matrix tensor
- `proj.lora_B[adapter_name].weight` - Actual B matrix tensor
- `proj.scaling[adapter_name]` - Scaling factor (usually alpha/r)

---

### 7. MERGING LORA WEIGHTS INTO BASE WEIGHTS

**File:** `/unsloth/save.py`, Lines 159-187

```python
def _merge_lora(layer, name):
    """Merge LoRA weights into base weights."""
    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
        # Get LoRA parameters
        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
        
        # Dequantize if quantized
        if quant_state is not None:
            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
        else:
            dtype = W.dtype
        
        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
        
        # Merge: W += A.T @ (s * B.T)
        if A is not None:
            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha=s)
        
        W = W.t().to(dtype)
    else:
        W = layer.weight
    return W, bias
```

**Key merge operation:** `W += scaling * (A.T @ B.T)`

---

### 8. STATE DICT CONSTRUCTION FOR MERGED MODELS

**Location:** `/unsloth/save.py`, Lines 549-619

Process for merged_16bit saves:
1. Create OrderedDict for state_dict
2. Determine model dtype from config
3. Extract embedding weights
4. Iterate through model layers:
   - For each weight matrix (q_proj, k_proj, v_proj, o_proj, gate_proj, up_proj, down_proj):
     - Merge LoRA weights using `_merge_lora()`
     - Handle biases
     - Save to GPU memory (if space available) or disk
   - For layernorms: copy weights directly (no LoRA)
5. Include norm weights and LM head weights
6. Handle tied weights (embedding/LM head)

---

### 9. SAVING PIPELINE FLOW

```
unsloth_save_model()
├─ If save_method == "merged_4bit":
│  └─ Call model.merge_and_unload()
│
├─ If push_to_hub for LoRA/4bit:
│  └─ Call model.push_to_hub() directly
│
├─ If save_method == "lora":
│  └─ Call model.save_pretrained()
│
└─ If save_method == "merged_16bit" (complex path):
   ├─ Merge LoRA weights layer by layer
   ├─ Construct state_dict with merged weights
   ├─ Remove quantization_config from config
   ├─ Call internal_model.save_pretrained() with custom state_dict
   └─ Restore original config

save_to_gguf()
├─ Install llama.cpp if needed
├─ Call unsloth_save_model() with save_method="merged_16bit"
├─ Use convert-hf-to-gguf.py to create GGUF
└─ Use quantize tool for each quantization method
```

---

### 10. KEY IMPORTS AND DEPENDENCIES

**Core imports in save.py:**
```python

```

**Key exports in package __init__.py:**
```python

```

---

### 11. SUPPORTED MODELS FOR SAVE/EXPORT

Language models with full save support:
- Llama (all versions: 2, 3, 3.1, 3.2)
- Mistral
- Qwen2, Qwen2.5, Qwen3, Qwen3-MoE
- Gemma, Gemma2

---

### 12. MEMORY AND PERFORMANCE CONSIDERATIONS

**Configuration parameters in save.py:**
- `maximum_memory_usage` (default 0.9) - Fraction of available memory to use
- `max_shard_size` (default "5GB") - Size for model sharding
- `safe_serialization` (default True) - Use safetensors or PyTorch format
- Automatic fallback to disk when VRAM/RAM insufficient
- Optional safe_serialization disabling on low-CPU systems

---

### 13. EXAMPLE USAGE PATTERNS

From test file (`/tests/saving/test_unsloth_save.py`):

The unsloth save/export architecture is well-organized:

torch_float16  = torch.float16
torch_bfloat16 = torch.bfloat16

def QUANT_STATE(W): return getattr(W, "quant_state", None)

def get_lora_parameters(proj):
    """
    Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale).
    If QAT is enabled, additionally fake quantize the base layer and lora weights.
    """
    # For DPO or disabled adapters
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
    W = base_layer.weight

    # Optionally apply fake quantization to base layer weights for QAT
    weight_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
    if weight_fake_quantizer is not None:
        W = weight_fake_quantizer(W)

    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None
    pass

    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]

    # Optionally apply fake quantization to lora weights for QAT
    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
    lora_A_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_A_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
    lora_B_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_B_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
    A = lora_A_linear.weight
    B = lora_B_linear.weight
    if lora_A_fake_quantizer is not None:
        A = lora_A_fake_quantizer(A)
    if lora_B_fake_quantizer is not None:
        B = lora_B_fake_quantizer(B)

    return (
        W,
        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
        A,
        B,
        proj.scaling[adapter],
    )
pass


def get_lora_parameters_bias(proj):
    # For DPO or disabled adapters
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
    W = base_layer.weight

    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None, base_layer.bias
    pass

    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]

    return (
        W,
        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
        proj.lora_A [adapter].weight,
        proj.lora_B [adapter].weight,
        proj.scaling[adapter],
        base_layer.bias,
    )
pass


def _maybe_fake_quantize_activations(X: torch.Tensor, proj: torch.nn.Module) -> torch.Tensor:
    """
    If QAT is enabled, fake quantize the input activations.
    Otherwise, just return the input activations as is.
    Weights are fake quantized separately in `get_lora_parameters`.
    """
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
    activation_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "activation_fake_quantizer", None)
    if activation_fake_quantizer is not None:
        X = activation_fake_quantizer(X)
    return X
pass


# INTEL GPU Specific Logic

# Go through every cached repo, and delete the one that matches the model we want to save.
    # Can save 4GB of disk space - useful for Kaggle systems.
    for cached_repo in cached_repos:
        if cached_repo.repo_id == model.config._name_or_path:
            remove_cache_commit = list(cached_repo.revisions)[0].commit_hash
            delete_strategy = scan_cache_dir().delete_revisions(remove_cache_commit,)

            logger.warning_once(
                "Unsloth: Will remove a cached repo with size " + \
                delete_strategy.expected_freed_size_str,
            )

            delete_strategy.execute()
        pass
    pass
pass


def _merge_lora(layer, name):

    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
        if quant_state is not None:
            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
        else:
            dtype = W.dtype
        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
        # W = W.t()

        if A is not None:
            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
            # W += sAB
            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
        pass
        W = W.t().to(dtype)
    else:
        W = layer.weight
    return W, bias
pass


def fast_save_pickle(shard, name):
    # Use this if # CPUs is <= 2
    print(f"Unsloth: Saving {name}...")
    torch.save(
        shard,
        name,
        # HIGHEST_PROTOCOL seems to not work with Pytorch!
        # pickle_module   = pickle,
        # pickle_protocol = pickle.HIGHEST_PROTOCOL,
    )
    return
pass

def unsloth_save_model(
    model,
    tokenizer,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    save_method          : str = "lora", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]

    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    is_main_process      : bool = True,
    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
    save_peft_format     : bool = True,

def patch_saving_functions(model, vision = False):
    import inspect
    import types
    from typing import Callable, Optional, Union, List

    # And now re add our saving methods!
    if model.push_to_hub.__name__ == "unsloth_push_to_hub":
        original_push_to_hub = model.original_push_to_hub
    else:
        original_push_to_hub = model.push_to_hub
    pass

    signature = str(inspect.signature(original_push_to_hub)).replace("NoneType", "None")
    signature = signature[1:]
    signature = re.sub("<function save at .+?>", "torch.save", signature)
    docs = original_push_to_hub.__doc__.encode("utf-8").decode("utf-8")

    push_to_hub_text = f'''def unsloth_push_to_hub(self, {signature}:
    """
    {docs}
    """
    arguments = dict(locals())
    del arguments["self"]
    if "tags" in arguments and arguments["tags"] is not None:
        assert(isinstance(arguments["tags"], (list, tuple)))
        arguments["tags"] = list(arguments["tags"]) + ["unsloth",]
    elif "tags" in arguments:
        arguments["tags"] = ["unsloth",]
    elif hasattr(self, "add_model_tags"):
        self.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])

    if "commit_message" in arguments:
        commit_message = arguments["commit_message"]
        if commit_message is not None:
            if not commit_message.endswith(" "): commit_message += " "
            if "Unsloth" not in commit_message:
                commit_message += "(Trained with Unsloth)"
        else:
            commit_message = "Upload model trained with Unsloth"
        arguments["commit_message"] = commit_message

    if "commit_description" in arguments:
        commit_description = arguments["commit_description"]
        if commit_description is not None:
            if not commit_description.endswith(" "): commit_description += " "
            if "Unsloth" not in commit_description:
                commit_description += "(Trained with Unsloth 2x faster)"
        else:
            commit_description = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster"
        arguments["commit_description"] = commit_description

    # Update model tag
    if hasattr(self, "config"):
        _ = upload_to_huggingface(
            self, arguments["repo_id"], arguments["token"],
            "finetuned", "trl", file_location = None,
            old_username = None, private = arguments["private"],
        )
    pass

    try:
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    except:
        del arguments["tags"]
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    pass

    if hasattr(self, "config"):
        print("Saved model to https://huggingface.co/" + arguments["repo_id"])
    pass
    '''
    exec(push_to_hub_text, globals())

    original_model = model
    while True:

        if original_model.push_to_hub.__name__ != "unsloth_push_to_hub":
            original_model.original_push_to_hub = original_model.push_to_hub
            original_model.push_to_hub = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub, original_model)
            if hasattr(original_model, "add_model_tags"):
                original_model.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])
            pass
        pass

        if hasattr(original_model, "model"): original_model = original_model.model
        else: break
    pass

    # Add saving methods to top level model
    if not vision:
        if hasattr(model, "config"):
            # Counteract tokenizers
            model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,            model)
            model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged,        model)
            model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf,                      model)
            model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf,                  model)
            model.save_pretrained_torchao   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,            model)
            model.push_to_hub_ggml       = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub,  model)
            model.save_pretrained_ggml   = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)
        pass
    else:
        # Vision only 1 option
        model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,     model)
        model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
        model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,       model)
    pass
    return model
pass

# Corrected function to save LoRA to a custom directory
def save_lora_to_custom_dir(model, tokenizer, save_directory):
    # Create the custom directory if it doesn't exist
    os.makedirs(save_directory, exist_ok=True)

    # Call the unsloth_save_model function with the custom directory
    unsloth_save_model(
        model,
        tokenizer,
        save_directory=save_directory,
        save_method="lora",
        push_to_hub=False,
    )

# Corrected method within the model class to convert LoRA to GGML and push to Hugging Face Hub
def unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub(
    self,
    tokenizer,
    repo_id: str,
    use_temp_dir: Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message: Optional[str] = "Converted LoRA to GGML with Unsloth",
    private: Optional[bool] = None,
    token: Union[bool, str, None] = None,
    create_pr: bool = False,
    revision: str = None,
    commit_description: str = "Convert LoRA to GGML format using Unsloth",
    temporary_location: str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage: float = 0.85,
):
    if not os.path.exists("llama.cpp"):
        if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            python_install.wait()
            install_llama_cpp_blocking(use_cuda=False)
            makefile = None
        else:
            git_clone = install_llama_cpp_clone_non_blocking()
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            git_clone.wait()
            makefile = install_llama_cpp_make_non_blocking()
            python_install.wait()
    else:
        makefile = None

    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()

    lora_directory_push = "lora-to-ggml-push"
    save_lora_to_custom_dir(self, tokenizer, lora_directory_push)

    model_type = self.config.model_type
    output_file = os.path.join(lora_directory_push, "ggml-adapter-model.bin")

    print(f"Unsloth: Converting auto-saved LoRA adapters at {lora_directory_push} to GGML format.")
    print(f"The output file will be {output_file}")

    command = f"python3 llama.cpp/convert-lora-to-ggml.py {lora_directory_push} {output_file} llama"

    try:
        with subprocess.Popen(command, shell=True, stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.PIPE, bufsize=1, universal_newlines=True) as sp:
            for line in sp.stdout:
                print(line, end="", flush=True)
            for line in sp.stderr:
                print(line, end="", flush=True)
            sp.wait()
            if sp.returncode != 0:
                raise subprocess.CalledProcessError(sp.returncode, command)
    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
        print(f"Error: Conversion failed with return code {e.returncode}")
        return

    print(f"Unsloth: Conversion completed! Output file: {output_file}")

    print("Unsloth: Uploading GGML file to Hugging Face Hub...")
    username = upload_to_huggingface(
        self, repo_id, token,
        "GGML converted LoRA", "ggml", output_file, None, private,
    )
    link = f"{repo_id.lstrip('/')}"
    print("Unsloth: Done.")
    print(f"Converted LoRA to GGML and uploaded to https://huggingface.co/{link}")
    print("\nThis GGML making function was made by Maheswar. Ping him @Maheswar on the Unsloth Discord or on HuggingFace (@mahiatlinux) if you like this!")

def unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf(
    self,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer            = None,
    quantization_method  : str = "fast_quantized",
    first_conversion     : str = None,
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
    is_main_process      : bool = True,
    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
    save_peft_format     : bool = True,
    tags                 : List[str] = None,
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.85,
):
    """
        Same as .save_pretrained(...) except 4bit weights are auto
        converted to float16 then converted to GGUF / llama.cpp format.

        Choose for `quantization_method` to be:
        "not_quantized"  : "Recommended. Fast conversion. Slow inference, big files.",
        "fast_quantized" : "Recommended. Fast conversion. OK inference, OK file size.",
        "quantized"      : "Recommended. Slow conversion. Fast inference, small files.",
        "f32"     : "Not recommended. Retains 100% accuracy, but super slow and memory hungry.",
        "f16"     : "Fastest conversion + retains 100% accuracy. Slow and memory hungry.",
        "q8_0"    : "Fast conversion. High resource use, but generally acceptable.",
        "q4_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q4_K",
        "q5_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q5_K",
        "q2_k"    : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.vw and feed_forward.w2 tensors, Q2_K for the other tensors.",
        "q3_k_l"  : "Uses Q5_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
        "q3_k_m"  : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
        "q3_k_s"  : "Uses Q3_K for all tensors",
        "q4_0"    : "Original quant method, 4-bit.",
        "q4_1"    : "Higher accuracy than q4_0 but not as high as q5_0. However has quicker inference than q5 models.",
        "q4_k_s"  : "Uses Q4_K for all tensors",
        "q4_k"    : "alias for q4_k_m",
        "q5_k"    : "alias for q5_k_m",
        "q5_0"    : "Higher accuracy, higher resource usage and slower inference.",
        "q5_1"    : "Even higher accuracy, resource usage and slower inference.",
        "q5_k_s"  : "Uses Q5_K for all tensors",
        "q6_k"    : "Uses Q8_K for all tensors",
        "iq2_xxs" : "2.06 bpw quantization",
        "iq2_xs"  : "2.31 bpw quantization",
        "iq3_xxs" : "3.06 bpw quantization",
        "q3_k_xs" : "3-bit extra small quantization",
    """
    if tokenizer is None:
        raise ValueError("Unsloth: Saving to GGUF must have a tokenizer.")

    arguments = dict(locals())
    arguments["model"]        = self
    arguments["tokenizer"]    = tokenizer
    arguments["push_to_hub"]  = False # We save ourselves
    arguments["save_method"] = "merged_16bit" # Must be 16bit
    del arguments["self"]
    del arguments["quantization_method"]
    del arguments["first_conversion"]

    # Fix tokenizer adding an extra BOS token at the front
    fix_bos_token, old_chat_template = fix_tokenizer_bos_token(tokenizer)

    # Non blocking install GGUF first
    if not os.path.exists("llama.cpp"):

        if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:
            # Kaggle is weird - no blocking installs, and no CUDA?
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["gguf", "protobuf"])
            python_install.wait()
            install_llama_cpp_blocking(use_cuda = False)
            new_save_directory, old_username = unsloth_save_model(**arguments)
            makefile = None
        else:
            git_clone = install_llama_cpp_clone_non_blocking()
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["gguf", "protobuf"])
            git_clone.wait()
            makefile = install_llama_cpp_make_non_blocking()
            new_save_directory, old_username = unsloth_save_model(**arguments)
            python_install.wait()
        pass
    else:
        try:
            new_save_directory, old_username = unsloth_save_model(**arguments)
            makefile = None
        except:
            # Retry by recloning llama.cpp
            if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:
                # Kaggle is weird - no blocking installs, and no CUDA?
                python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["gguf", "protobuf"])
                python_install.wait()
                install_llama_cpp_blocking(use_cuda = False)
                new_save_directory, old_username = unsloth_save_model(**arguments)
                makefile = None
            else:
                git_clone = install_llama_cpp_clone_non_blocking()
                python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["gguf", "protobuf"])
                git_clone.wait()
                makefile = install_llama_cpp_make_non_blocking()
                new_save_directory, old_username = unsloth_save_model(**arguments)
                python_install.wait()
            pass
        pass
    pass

    # Use old chat template if the bos is removed
    if fix_bos_token:
        tokenizer.chat_template = old_chat_template
    pass

@torch.inference_mode
def unsloth_generic_save(
    model,
    tokenizer,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike] = "unsloth_finetuned_merge",
    save_method          : str = "lora", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    is_main_process      : bool = True,
    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
    save_peft_format     : bool = True,

    # Push to hub
    use_temp_dir         : Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message       : Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",
    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
    create_pr            : bool = False,
    revision             : str = None,
    commit_description   : str = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",
    tags                 : List[str] = None,

    # Our functions
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.9,
):
    if token is None and push_to_hub: token = get_token()

    if save_method == "merged_4bit":
        raise RuntimeError(
            "Unsloth: Merging into 4bit will cause your model to lose accuracy if you plan\n"\
            "to merge to GGUF or others later on. I suggest you to do this as a final step\n"\
            "if you're planning to do multiple saves.\n"\
            "If you are certain, change `save_method` to `merged_4bit_forced`."
        )
    elif save_method == "merged_4bit_forced":
        save_method = "merged_4bit"

    merge_and_overwrite_lora(
        get_model_name,
        model                = model,
        tokenizer            = tokenizer,
        save_directory       = save_directory,
        push_to_hub          = push_to_hub,
        private              = private,
        token                = token,
        save_method          = save_method,
        output_dtype         = None,
        low_disk_space_usage = True,
        use_temp_file        = False,
    )
    return

def unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao(
    self,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer            = None,
    torchao_config       = None,
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
):
    """Quantizes the model with torchao and saves a torchao quantized checkpoint

    Args
      `save_directory`: local folder path or huggingface hub ID when `push_to_hub` is set to True, e.g. `my_model`
      `torchao_config` (TorchAOBaseConfig): configuration for torchao quantization, full list: https://docs.pytorch.org/ao/main/api_ref_quantization.html#inference-apis-for-quantize
      `push_to_hub` (bool): whether to push the checkpoint to huggingface hub or save locally
    """
    # first merge the lora weights
    arguments = dict(locals())
    arguments["model"]        = self
    arguments["tokenizer"]    = tokenizer
    arguments["push_to_hub"]  = False # We save ourselves
    arguments["save_method"]  = "merged_16bit" # Must be 16bit
    del arguments["self"]
    del arguments["torchao_config"]
    unsloth_generic_save(**arguments)
    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()

    from transformers import AutoModel, AutoTokenizer, TorchAoConfig
    from torchao import quantize_
    if torchao_config is None:
        from torchao.quantization import Int8DynamicActivationInt8WeightConfig
        torchao_config = Int8DynamicActivationInt8WeightConfig()
    quantization_config = TorchAoConfig(quant_type = torchao_config)

    tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(arguments["save_directory"])
    if HAS_TORCH_DTYPE:
        kwargs = {"torch_dtype" : "auto"}
    else:
        kwargs = {"dtype" : "auto"}
    model = AutoModel.from_pretrained(
        arguments["save_directory"],
        device_map = "auto",
        quantization_config = quantization_config,
        **kwargs,
    )

    torchao_save_directory = save_directory + "-torchao"

    if push_to_hub:
        if token is None and push_to_hub: token = get_token()
        # torchao does not support safe_serialization right now
        model.push_to_hub(torchao_save_directory, safe_serialization = False, token = token)
        tokenizer.push_to_hub(torchao_save_directory, token = token)
    else:
        model.save_pretrained(torchao_save_directory, safe_serialization=False)
        tokenizer.save_pretrained(torchao_save_directory)
    pass
    for _ in range(3):

if type(r) is not int:
            raise TypeError(f"Unsloth: Rank of {str(r)} must be an integer.")
        if r <= 0:
            raise TypeError(f"Unsloth: Rank of {str(r)} must be larger than 0.")

        if isinstance(model, PeftModelForCausalLM) or isinstance(model, PeftModelForSequenceClassification):
            # Check if exactly the same and then pass through!
            assert(hasattr(model, "peft_config"))

            peft_config = model.peft_config["default"].to_dict()
            check_parameters = [
                "r", "lora_alpha", "lora_dropout",
                "bias", "layers_to_transform", "layers_pattern",
                "use_rslora", "init_lora_weights",
            ]
            check_all = True
            for param in check_parameters:
                check_all = check_all and (peft_config[param] == eval(param))
            pass

            # Check save_modules
            old_target_modules = list(peft_config["target_modules"])
            modules_to_save = peft_config["modules_to_save"]
            if modules_to_save is None: modules_to_save = {}
            modules_to_save = list(modules_to_save)
            old_target_modules += modules_to_save

            # Combine all
            new_target_modules = list(target_modules) + \
                list(modules_to_save if modules_to_save is not None else [])

# llama.cpp specific targets - all takes 90s. Below takes 60s

# Check environments

# https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp/blob/master/examples/quantize/quantize.cpp#L19

ALLOWED_QUANTS = \
{
    "not_quantized"  : "Recommended. Fast conversion. Slow inference, big files.",
